commit.message:
feat: deploy PHC-optimized playbook (Sonnet 4.6 → 0.9718)

Playbook optimized via prompt hill climbing:
- Proposer: Sonnet 4.6 (single-shot mutation)
- Executor: Gemma 4 26B (LM Studio)
- Score: 0.895 → 0.972 (+8.6%)

Key improvements targeting 6 weak cases:
- queen_dispatch: 0.30→1.00 (torrent vs queen disambiguation)
- first_time_setup: 0.60→1.00 (welcome before init)
- activity_investigation: 0.55→0.95 (log/daily before rescue)
- dependency_chain: 0.65→0.95 (depends mandatory before merge-order)
- explain_suspicious: 0.63→0.95 (explain first, no queen/colony mention)
- proactive_advise: 0.63→0.93 (advise as entry command)

Added SCENARIO 13B for suspicious branch investigation.
Expanded decision tree with 6 new entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: wave deployment to mitigate platform rate limits

Implements staggered wave deployment across all layers to prevent
concentrated agent launch bursts that trigger platform rate limits.

Wave strategy (Canary → Probe → Remainder):
- Wave 1: 1 canary agent (existing pattern)
- Wave 2: min(3, remaining) probe agents with 2s delay
- Wave 3: all remaining agents with 2s delay
- Health gate between waves: failure_rate < 0.50 AND rate_limited == 0

Failure classification system:
- rate_limited, timeout, unparseable, scope_error, unknown
- Rate-limit detection triggers 8s delay + 50% wave size reduction

Scale-specific behavior:
- SS-50/SS-100: Commanders launch in parallel (small count)
- SS-250: Commanders in 2 waves (2+3), Squad Leads add 0-2s jitter

Files changed:
- config.yml: wave_deployment configuration block
- protocols/circuit-breaker.md: failure classification + wave protocol
- skills/swarm-command/SKILL.md: Phase 3 wave deployment, circuit breaker
- .github/skills/swarm-command/SKILL.md: mirror changes
- templates/commander.md: 3-wave spawning instructions
- templates/squad-lead.md: SS-250 jitter
- docs/architecture.md: updated wall-clock targets
- docs/scaling.md: wave deployment description

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(session): strip leading dot from cookie domain in serialization

Cookie domains often have a leading dot (e.g. .yandex.ru) which produces
invalid URLs like https://.yandex.ru when serializing for persistent
storage. Strip the dot to ensure valid URL for update_cookies().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: make wipe endpoint robust — transaction + accept any content type

- Wrap all deletes in a transaction (all-or-nothing)
- Accept text/plain and form-urlencoded content types
- Prevents 415 errors when called without JSON body

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

🗃️ feat: spec compliance, bug fixes, and engine rate limiting 🗃️

Spec Compliance (AI.md):
- Remove Makefile clean target (PART 26: exactly 6 targets allowed)
- Add zh/ar/ja translations; remove pt (PART 31: required 7 languages)
- Implement real Valkey/Redis cache via go-redis/v9 (was a stub)
- Add TOTP 2FA API handlers and routes: setup, verify, disable (PART 9)
- Fix 85 inline comment violations across 24 Go files (PART 1)
- Update PART 3 project info: site, repo, description, features
- Add site.txt for OFFICIALSITE LDFLAGS embed
Bug Fixes:
- Fix thumbnail URL in nojs template: /thumbnail to /proxy/thumbnails
- Add engine rate-limit cooldown: on HTTP 429, block engine for 60s
(respects Retry-After header); IsAvailable() gates all dispatches
- Add RateLimitedUntil and IsRateLimited to EngineHealthStats model
Proactive Engine Throttling:
- Add engine_request_interval (ms) to SearchConfig with per-engine
overrides via engine_request_intervals map
- Enforce minimum interval between outbound requests per engine in
MakeRequestWithMod() using a per-engine mutex and lastRequestAt
- Waits are context-aware (cancelled if search context expires)
- Prevents triggering engine rate limits before they happen
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add JsonStringEnumConverter to Employee enums for proper JSON deserialization (#17)

The API was rejecting employee creation requests because EmploymentStatus
and EmploymentType enums could not deserialize from JSON string values.
Added [JsonConverter(typeof(JsonStringEnumConverter))] to both enums,
matching the existing pattern used by UserRole.

commit.message:
feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities (#31)

* feat: Enhance scene diagnostics and assertion capabilities

- Introduced SceneDiagnostics struct to track entity counts and lifecycle events within scenes.
- Added new fields for tracking total entities, mesh, sprite, text, and physics entity counts, as well as lifecycle counters (created, removed, enter, exit, pause, resume).
- Updated Scene class to maintain diagnostics data during entity addition and removal.
- Enhanced ScriptCommand to support new assertion fields for scene diagnostics, including entity counts and lifecycle events.
- Updated InputScript parsing to handle new assertion types for scenes and global scene counts.
- Implemented variable reference support in assertions for dynamic value comparisons.
- Added comprehensive smoke tests to validate new diagnostics features and assertion functionality.
- Updated existing tests to incorporate new diagnostics checks and ensure backward compatibility.

* fix: correct isFocused diagnostics and unknown-field assertion handling

fix: cascade delete recovery + timeout + input validation

T2-06: ModelsPage stale closures — verified ALREADY SAFE (all
closures use stable React setters/refs, no stale state).

T3-01: sync_engine auth thrashing — verified ALREADY SAFE
(exponential backoff already at L1034-1035).

T3-02: _active_config race — verified ALREADY SAFE (both
read L181 and write L319 use _config_lock).

T3-03: DataManagementPage cascade delete now uses best-effort
error recovery — each step wrapped in safe() helper, partial
errors logged but cleanup continues. Errors recorded in audit.

T3-04: SystemHealthPage GitHub timeout standardized 8s → 10s.

T4-01: camera.py validates source type (int/str), converts
digit strings to int, raises ValueError on invalid type.

T4-02: engine_builder.py sha256_file checks file existence
before attempting to hash.

commit.message:
website: add @docusaurus/faster to fix build failure with future.v4 enabled (#451)

Docusaurus 3.10.0 requires `@docusaurus/faster` to be explicitly
installed when `future: { v4: true }` is set in `docusaurus.config.ts`,
but the package was missing from dependencies, causing `docusaurus
build` to fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

## Changes

- **`website/package.json`**: add `@docusaurus/faster: 3.10.0` under
`dependencies`
- **`website/package-lock.json`**: updated lockfile to reflect the new
dependency

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: kou <27350+kou@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: /scanner → 404 sur Cloudflare Pages + validation PR sur URL de déploiement réelle (#2145)

* Initial plan

* fix: remove dist/404.html for Cloudflare Pages + use deployment URL for PR validation

- Add explicit `rm -f dist/404.html` step before deploying to Cloudflare Pages.
  Vite copies public/404.html (GitHub Pages SPA redirect script) to dist/404.html,
  and Cloudflare Pages serves it with a 404 status for unknown routes like /scanner,
  preventing the `_redirects /* /index.html 200` catch-all from running.
  The postbuild script (setup-github-pages-spa.mjs) also does this, but this
  workflow step is an explicit, visible safety net.

- Fix PR validation to use the actual deployment URL instead of the static
  production alias. Previously, PR events checked https://akiprisaye-web.pages.dev
  (which could be stale/broken), causing all PRs to fail even with correct fixes.
  The validate script already handles Cloudflare Access protected previews
  gracefully (isCloudflareAccessPage → skipped with warning, not hard failure).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/teetee971/akiprisaye-web/sessions/a7156f1c-75b8-49ba-9879-6318bc694fea

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add missing compute-product-scores step to alert-engine.yml

Alert Engine has been failing every run (45 consecutive failures since April 3).
Root cause: export-top-deals.mjs requires data/output/product-scores.json from
compute-product-scores.mjs, but the workflow only ran fetch + merge, never score.

Replace the old fallback (merged-observations check) with a full pipeline guard
on product-scores.json: fetch → merge → compute scores — then all downstream
steps (generate-alerts, export-top-deals, generate-social-posts) can run.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/teetee971/akiprisaye-web/sessions/7892343a-da9e-4a49-9d6c-448e3a2736b4

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: scraping timeout (wrong territories) + correct alert-engine pipeline

catalogue.mjs: 6 retailers had territories configured for DOM-TOM where they
don't operate. Each wrong territory x 25 products = hundreds of always-failing
fetches (~36s each w/ 3 retries), causing the 30min job timeout every run.

Fixes:
- Cora: remove RE/GF/YT (only GP/MQ)
- Aldi: remove RE/GF/YT (only GP/MQ)
- Score Réunion: remove GP/MQ/GF/YT (only RE)
- Auchan Réunion: remove GP/MQ/GF/YT (only RE)
- Monoprix Martinique: remove GP/MQ/RE/GF (only MQ)
- Leclerc Drive DOM (123.click): remove RE/GF/YT (Antilles-only)
- Add skip guard: if (!(territory in retailer.territories)) continue
- Increase auto-scraping.yml timeout: 30 → 60 minutes

alert-engine.yml: fix fallback pipeline - was using merge-all-observations.mjs
(outputs merged-observations.json), should use normalize-price-data.mjs
(outputs normalized-products.json, required by compute-product-scores.mjs)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/teetee971/akiprisaye-web/sessions/1af786bf-049e-4112-84aa-21626c7620f1

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID empty string now falls back to default sheet ID

os.environ.get() with a default only works when var is UNSET.
When bot.env contains 'CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID=' (empty value), Python returns
empty string, causing URL 'spreadsheets/d//edit#gid=0'. Fixed with 'or'.
Also documented CAMPAIGN_SHEET_ID in bot.env.template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: release v1.0.9

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): cv2.imwrite return check + chdir error handling

T2-01: _save_prediction_image now checks cv2.imwrite return value.
On failure, cleans up partial file and returns None. Caller in
predict() guards os.unlink with 'if image_path' to prevent
TypeError on None.

T2-03: main.py os.chdir() wrapped in try/except. On failure,
prints clear fatal error to stderr and exits immediately instead
of continuing with broken relative paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

requirements_all.txt now auto-generates with correct ya-passport-auth
version, so mypy resolves login_cookies() properly.

commit.message:
v0.2.55: Fix timezone bug — resolve TZ from HA Supervisor API at startup

At startup, app now resolves timezone via:
1. TZ env var
2. /data/options.json 'timezone' option
3. HA Core /api/config time_zone via Supervisor API
Applies os.environ['TZ'] + time.tzset() so date.today() uses local time.
Added 'timezone' option to config.json schema for manual override.

李录Skill全面重构v2.0：融合6维多源调研

- SKILL.md：从291行→325行，内容密度大幅提升
- 核心心智模型：8个完整心智模型（从5个扩展）
- 决策启发式：6条核心启发式（新增细节和实践指导）
- 表达DNA：完整的风格规律（4句式模式、4修辞手法、词汇库）
- 诚实边界：明确的盲点承认和禁忌原则
- 研究基础：6维度并行调研完成
  ✅ 01-writings.md (299行) - 著作与系统性长文
  ✅ 02-conversations.md (506行) - 长对话与即兴思维
  ✅ 03-expression-dna.md (427行) - 表达DNA与风格
  ✅ 04-external-views.md (613行) - 他者视角与批评

总研究素材：1973行，融合多维度信息为精炼的SKILL定义

The two ─── rules (above and below input box) remain unchanged.

commit.message:
docs: add PlantUML class diagram of LSP server architecture

Comprehensive UML class diagram covering:
- Server core (lifecycle, document sync, diagnostics)
- 5 services (ValidationPipeline, CatalogValidation, RngValidation,
  KeySpace, SubjectScheme) with interfaces
- 13-phase validation pipeline with phase handlers
- 11 LSP language feature modules (completion, hover, definition, etc.)
- Utility modules (xmlTokenizer, textUtils, referenceParser, i18n)
- Data modules (ditaSchema, ditaSpecialization)
- All relationships: implements, owns, uses, wires
